summary: Small matching-oriented cleanup across ftCommon and related library types. The PR adjusted Bury physics field usage and min/max expression shape, reordered locals and expression spelling in FlyReflect, introduced a temporary index variable in ftcolanim dispatch, and corrected DynamicsDesc.count from signed int to unsigned int. Evidence is limited to the diff; no substantive review discussion was included in the slice.
what_changed: In ftCo_Bury.c, added a local MY_MIN macro and changed ftCo_Bury_Phys to compute mv_x18_x from MY_MIN(box->right.y, box->left.y) instead of MAX over coll_box.right.x/right.y.
In ftCo_Bury.c, changed the bottom adjustment from adding fp->mv.co.bury.coll_box.right.x to adding fp->mv.co.bury.x1C.
In ftCo_FlyReflect.c, reordered locals in ftCo_800C18A8 so the temporary byte array is declared after Vec3 vec1 and Vec3 vec0.
In ftCo_FlyReflect.c, changed efAsync_Spawn's second argument from &fp->x60C to &GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->x60C, preserving behavior but changing expression form.
In ftCo_FlyReflect.c, changed facing_dir assignment literals from integer -1/1 to float -1.0F/1.0F.
In ftcolanim.c, split ftCo_803C6ADC[arg0 - 0x15](gobj, cmd) into int i = arg0 - 21; ftCo_803C6ADC[i](gobj, cmd);.
In lb/types.h, changed struct DynamicsDesc field count from int to unsigned int.
smart_moves: Used expression reshaping rather than broad logic changes, keeping the patch small while targeting codegen-sensitive differences.
Introduced an intermediate index variable in ft_800BFF70, a common decomp tactic when direct array-index expressions do not match the original instruction scheduling or register allocation.
Adjusted literal types for facing_dir to float constants, aligning source semantics with a float field and likely avoiding unwanted integer-to-float codegen shape.
Corrected a shared header field to unsigned int, improving type accuracy for DynamicsDesc consumers without touching call sites in this slice.
Replaced repeated deep field access in ftCo_Bury_Phys with a local ftCollisionBox* alias for the min computation, making the intended struct fields clearer.
decomp_lessons: For matching work, equivalent C expressions can produce different code; preserving behavior is not enough when the compiler's local layout, expression tree, and literal types matter.
When a struct field is known to be nonnegative or count-like, update the header type directly; signedness in shared types can affect comparisons, loads, and downstream matches.
Float fields should generally be assigned float literals such as -1.0F and 1.0F rather than integer literals when trying to reproduce original codegen.
Temporary variables can be useful even for simple arithmetic like arg0 - 21 if they reproduce the original register lifetime or indexing pattern.
Local declaration order can matter for stack layout; moving a dummy byte array after Vec3 locals is a plausible matching fix.
naming_conventions: Existing anonymous or provisional function names were retained, including ftCo_800C18A8, ft_800BFF70, ftCo_803C6ADC, and Camera_80030E44.
The new macro was named MY_MIN rather than a domain-specific name, suggesting it was introduced as a local codegen aid rather than a public utility.
The temporary dispatch index in ft_800BFF70 was named simply i, matching a minimal decomp style for short-lived arithmetic temporaries.
The header struct field name count was retained while only its signedness changed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reordered local declarations in ftCo_800C18A8 to influence stack layout.
Used a dummy initialized byte array u8 _[8] = { 0 } after Vec3 locals, likely to preserve stack allocation or initialization behavior.
Changed &fp->x60C to &GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->x60C to alter reload or aliasing/codegen shape without changing the effective object.
Changed numeric constants from 0x15 to decimal 21 in the split index expression; the semantic value is unchanged, but source shape may affect readability or matching.
Introduced MY_MIN and changed selected Bury collision-box fields, indicating a correction to both expression form and likely field interpretation.
Changed signed int to unsigned int for DynamicsDesc.count, a type-level matching tactic that can affect generated comparisons and loads.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR context reports one issue comment, but no human text excerpt was available, so no actionable reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftCo_Bury_Phys or ftCo_800C0FCC to see whether MY_MIN was retained or replaced by a shared macro.
Search for DynamicsDesc.count users to confirm unsigned int did not require additional signedness fixes.
Compare ftCo_800C18A8 in later revisions for whether the GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->x60C expression remained necessary for matching.
Look for other ftCo_803C6ADC dispatch changes using intermediate index variables as a matching pattern.
